title: On the So-Called Reading Crisis as Class Warfare
description: “A book is a powerful weapon, it can defend you in your mind.” —Lorenzo Kom’boa Ervin French theorist Pierre Bourdieu once described photography as the art of the middle class. The wealthy know expensive paintings, the lower class comics, and
hook: How reading became a class battleground: Bourdieu's theory of cultural capital reveals why we fight over books.

title: What’s So Funny About Infinite Jest?
description: “Readers now seem eager to leave behind the book’s ‘litbro’ baggage and to engage with this complicated, pleasurable novel on its own terms.”
hook: Reclaiming Infinite Jest from 'litbro' baggage to engage with its genuine complexity and pleasure as literature.

title: 7 Novels About Women Becoming Beasts
description:
There are days lately when my body feels too small for everything I’m feeling. Maybe you know the sensation. That hot, tight coil of frustration that won’t unwind. The pressure of trying to stay pleasant while the world around you keeps insisting you should be grateful it’s not worse. It’s a strange kind of claustrophobia: […]

The post 7 Novels About Women Becoming Beasts appeared first on
hook: Seven novels exploring women's rage through transformation into beasts—a visceral meditation on female interiority and fury.

title: The Testament of Ann Lee review – expands the possibilities of filmed biography
description: Mona Fastvold creates a bold and intoxicating meta-musical that chronicles the eventful life and times of Ann Lee, one of the founders of religious sect the Shakers.
hook: Bold meta-musical biography that expands the form itself: how Mona Fastvold chronicles Ann Lee and the Shakers.

title: Frederick Wiseman Always Made His Point
description: The late filmmaker captured our essential American institutions—and the people trying to navigate them.
hook: Frederick Wiseman's essential institutions captured on film: a meditation on American life through observational cinema.
